title: US PPI Forecast Snapshot — Composite Calibrated Bridge Nowcast v2.5 Enhanced
summary: June 2026 PPI (release Wed Jul 15, 08:30 ET): Headline +0.1% MoM, Core +0.3% MoM, YoY ~6.2%/5.0%. VIX 17.16 (Jul 13, +14.2% intraday on US-Iran re-escalation) keeps the model in Normal regime but borderline — any push above 18 flips parameters to Elevated. June energy deflation is the story: AAA regular $3.872 vs $4.086 a month ago (-5.2%), diesel $4.875 vs $5.236 (-6.9%), with the June PPI sampling window (week of Jun 9) catching prices after the early-June peak but before the late-June crash — partial passthrough. ISM Prices Paid 73.0 (normalized +2.3 ppt) and ECI Q1 +0.9% SA (+0.30 monthly proxy) keep core sticky. EIA w/e Jul 3: crude +3.0M (411.4M) but distillate -5.0M to 103.6M (12% below 5-yr avg) — fuel-supply crunch persists. CRITICAL release-day risk: US-Iran strikes over the weekend sent WTI +9% to $77.85 and Brent to $79.11; Iran claims Hormuz shut (6 vessels Sunday, fewest in 5 weeks). This hits JULY PPI, not June — but it warps market reaction function: a hot surprise tomorrow now gets amplified, a cool one discounted. CPI lands today 08:30 ET and sets the pre-PPI tape.
baseCase: Headline PPI +0.1% MoM, Core PPI +0.3% MoM. Energy composite -3.6% as June refined-product deflation passes through partially (sampling window mid-month). Core goods +0.45% anchored by ISM Prices Paid 73.0. Services sticky at +0.21% via ECI labor cost passthrough. Intermediate demand +0.15 ppt uplift (Stage1 +3.2%, Stage2 +2.4%). YoY cools to ~6.2% from 6.5%.

release.name: PPI
release.thesis: June's energy collapse (Brent -23% in June, retail gasoline -5% MoM, diesel -7% MoM) drags headline toward flat, but the mid-month sampling window limits full passthrough while ISM Prices Paid 73.0 and ECI-anchored services keep core sticky near +0.3%. Prediction markets price June YoY ~6.2-6.4%, in line with our 6.2%. Weekend US-Iran re-escalation (WTI +9% to $77.85, VIX +14% to 17.16) is a July-print problem, but it steepens tomorrow's reaction function: hot surprises get amplified, cool ones faded.

scenarios.label: Base Case — Energy Drag vs Sticky Core
scenarios.outcome: Headline PPI +0.1% MoM, Core PPI +0.3% MoM, YoY 6.2%/5.0%. June energy deflation offsets sticky core goods (ISM 73.0) and ECI-anchored services.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: YoY cooling from 6.5% gives the doves oxygen, but war premium in oil and today's CPI print dominate. 10Y whipsaws on geopolitics, not on a +0.1% headline.
scenarios.invalidation: Core services print above +0.5% MoM; May revisions push prior core above 5.3% YoY

scenarios.label: Hot Case — Sampling Catches the Peak
scenarios.outcome: Headline PPI +0.4% MoM, Core PPI +0.5% MoM. Mid-June sampling caught elevated pre-crash energy; ISM 73 passthrough to core goods; trade services margins widen.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: Stacks on top of the war bid in crude. Hawkish repricing: 2Y Treasury jumps, USD firms, equities sell off — especially with CPI risk 24h earlier.
scenarios.invalidation: Energy component prints below -4% MoM, confirming full June crash passthrough

scenarios.label: Cool Case — Full Energy Passthrough
scenarios.outcome: Headline PPI -0.2% MoM, Core PPI +0.1% MoM. June crash fully passes through the sampling window; wholesale margin compression; services ex-trade flat.
scenarios.marketReadthrough: YoY slides under 6.0%, rate-hike pricing unwinds at the margin. But the bid in crude from Iran caps the rally in duration — TLT fades after the first pop.
scenarios.invalidation: Core goods print above +0.6% on tariff/ISM passthrough; ECI-driven services upside

setups.bias: Tactical long only on cool core + benign CPI; otherwise war premium keeps yields bid
setups.trigger: Core PPI <= +0.2% and June CPI core <= +0.2%
setups.invalidation: Hot core stack (CPI+PPI both hot) or Hormuz escalation pushing WTI > $85

setups.bias: Long volatility / long on escalation — distillate crunch amplifies any supply scare
setups.trigger: Iran ceasefire frays further; EIA distillate stocks stay 12%+ below 5-yr avg; WTI holds > $75
setups.invalidation: Formal ceasefire + OPEC+ adds; WTI back under $70

setups.bias: Long as geopolitical hedge into the CPI/PPI double-header
setups.trigger: Hot inflation + war escalation = stagflation bid; DXY stalls at 101
setups.invalidation: Cool CPI+PPI stack AND ceasefire signed — real yields pop, gold de-rates

setups.bias: Long on hot PPI surprise with rates follow-through
setups.trigger: Headline and core both beat; terminal-rate pricing lifts
setups.invalidation: Rates fade despite the beat; risk-off bid goes to gold/CHF instead

watchItems.label: US-Iran / Hormuz (dominant risk)
watchItems.value: WTI $77.85 (+9% Jul 13); Brent $79.11; 6 vessels Sunday — fewest in 5 weeks
watchItems.note: July-print risk, but defines tomorrow's reaction function. Iran claims strait shut; Trump says shipping open.

watchItems.label: June CPI (today 08:30 ET)
watchItems.note: Sets the pre-PPI tape. Cleveland Fed nowcast: CPI 4.05%, core 2.86%.

watchItems.label: EIA fuel-supply crunch
watchItems.note: Products tight even as crude builds. Russia diesel export ban + China export uncertainty widen the crude-products divide.

watchItems.label: ISM Prices Paid
watchItems.note: Normalized +2.3 ppt in core goods composite. Still strongly inflationary; July print Aug 1.

watchItems.label: BLS ECI Q2 2026
watchItems.note: Best services proxy. Q2 >1.0% would lift the Other Services composite for Q3 prints.

watchItems.label: VIX regime boundary
watchItems.note: A close above 18 flips the model to Elevated regime (bias window 2mo, wider CI).

keyDrivers: June energy deflation is real but partially sampled: AAA regular -5.2% MoM, diesel -6.9% MoM; the Jun 9-week sampling window caught mid-crash prices — expect energy drag of ~-0.25 to -0.30 ppt on headline, not the full crash.
Core stickiness is structural: ISM Prices Paid 73.0 (+2.3 ppt normalized) + ECI Q1 +0.9% SA (+0.30 monthly proxy) keep core PPI near +0.3% MoM regardless of energy.
War re-escalation changes the asymmetry, not the June print: WTI +9% to $77.85 on US-Iran strikes means a hot PPI tomorrow gets amplified (stagflation), a cool one gets faded as stale.
Fuel-supply crunch under the surface: distillate stocks 12% below 5-yr average at 103.6M bbl while crude builds — refined products stay bid even if crude softens, supporting July energy PPI.
Pipeline pressure still live: intermediate demand Stage1 +3.2%, Stage2 +2.4% adds +0.15 ppt uplift — upstream inflation has not cleared.
risks: Upside — sampling-window trap: if BLS sampling caught the Jun 3-13 peak (WTI $84-98), energy prints near flat and headline jumps to +0.4%.
Upside — Hormuz escalation between now and release: market stops caring about backward-looking June data entirely; any non-cool print triggers disorderly stagflation repricing.
Downside — full June crash passthrough: if the index catches late-June prices (WTI $72), headline prints negative and YoY slides under 6.0%.
Downside — CPI first-mover: a cool CPI today could pre-commit the market to fading tomorrow's PPI regardless of the print.
Revision risk: May's +1.1% MoM / 6.5% YoY can be revised; a higher revised base changes the YoY math by ~0.2 ppt.
sources: https://gasprices.aaa.com/ (AAA regular $3.872, diesel $4.875, Jul 13 2026; month ago $4.086/$5.236)
https://crudesignal.io/crude-oil-inventory-report (EIA w/e Jul 3: crude +3.0M to 411.4M, gasoline -1.9M, distillate -5.0M to 103.6M, SPR -6.2M)
https://tanktransport.com/2026/07/fuel-supply-crunch/ (EIA WPSR Jul 8: distillate 12% below 5-yr avg, utilization 95.8%)
https://octagonai.co/markets/economics/inflation/ppi-yoy-in-june/ (June PPI YoY prediction market: 56% >6.2%, 44% >6.4%; May +1.1% MoM/6.5% YoY, energy +10.7%, gasoline +23.4%)
https://www.cmelitegroup.com/knowledge-hub/cpi-report-release-time-schedule-latest-data-and-how-it-moves-markets/ (CPI Jul 14, PPI Jul 15, Retail Jul 16; May CPI +0.5%/4.2%, core +0.2%/2.9%)
